name: understand-domain
tagline: Scans a codebase (or reuses a cached knowledge graph) to extract business-domain flows and render them in an interactive dashboard.
verdict: Clean, local-only skill: no credential access, no network egress, no remote/fetched-code execution, no privilege escalation. Steps are concrete, idempotent, and fail gracefully. Minor gaps only: no negative-trigger guidance, no declared tooling/version metadata, and it auto-deletes its own generated intermediate files (low risk, self-generated data only).

highlights.summary: No explicit negative-trigger guidance ('do not use when...') anywhere in the file — a user can't tell from SKILL.md when this tool is the wrong choice.
No example domain-graph output or example entry-point detection is shown, though the procedural steps are detailed enough to follow without one.
Frontmatter declares only name/description/argument-hint — no tools/permissions/dependency field — yet the skill requires python3, git, and bash to be present and on PATH.
